programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: When a virus leaks from a top-secret facility, turning all resident researchers into ravenous zombies and their lab animals into mutated hounds from hell, the government sends in an elite military task force to contain the outbreak.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Ten years after the events of the original, a reprogrammed T-800 is sent back in time to protect young John Connor from the shape-shifting T-1000. Together with his mother Sarah, he fights to stop Skynet from triggering a nuclear apocalypse.

programme.title: Every Which Way But Loose
programme.desc: Philo Beddoe is your regular, easygoing, truck-driving guy. He's also the best bar-room brawler west of the Rockies. And he lives with a 165-pound orangutan named Clyde. Like other guys, Philo finally falls in love - with a flighty singer who leads him on a screwball chase across the American Southwest. Nothing's in the way except a motorcycle gang, some cops, and legendary brawler Tank Murdock.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: In a future where a failed global-warming experiment kills off most life on the planet, a class system evolves aboard the Snowpiercer; a train that travels around the globe via a perpetual-motion engine.

programme.title: Predator
programme.desc: A team of commandos on a mission in a Central American jungle find themselves hunted by an extraterrestrial warrior.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: In this third installment of the adrenaline-fueled action franchise, skilled assassin John Wick (Keanu Reeves) returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. After killing a member of the shadowy international assassin's guild, the High Table, John Wick is excommunicado, but the world's most ruthless hit men and women await his every turn.

programme.title: Pitch Black
programme.desc: A transport ship crashes and leaves its crew stranded on a desert planet inhabited by bloodthirsty creatures that come out during an eclipse.

programme.title: The League of Extraordinary Gentlemen
programme.desc: Renowned adventurer Allan Quatermain leads a team of extraordinary figures with legendary powers to battle the technological terror of a madman known as "The Fantom". This "League" comprises seafarer and inventor Captain Nemo, vampire Mina Harker, an invisible man named Rodney Skinner, American Secret Service Agent Tom Sawyer, the ageless and invincible Dorian Gray, and the dangerous split personality of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: After being bitten by a genetically-modified spider, a shy teenager gains spider-like abilities that he uses to fight injustice as a masked superhero and face a vengeful enemy.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: After a faulty interview with the Life Foundation ruins his career, former reporter Eddie Brock's life is in pieces. Six months later, he comes across the Life Foundation again, and he comes into contact with an alien symbiote and becomes Venom, a parasitic antihero.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
programme.desc: John Wick is forced out of retirement by a former associate looking to seize control of a shadowy international assassins’ guild. Bound by a blood oath to aid him, Wick travels to Rome and does battle against some of the world’s most dangerous killers.

programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: After success cleaning up Dodge City, Wyatt Earp moves to Tombstone, Arizona, and wishes to get rich in obscurity. He meets his brothers there, as well as his old friend Doc Holliday. A band of outlaws that call themselves The Cowboys are causing problems in the region with various acts of random violence, and inevitably come into confrontation with Holliday and the Earps, which leads to a shoot-out at the O.K. Corral.

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail 1974
programme.desc: King Arthur, accompanied by his squire, recruits his Knights of the Round Table, including Sir Bedevere the Wise, Sir Lancelot the Brave, Sir Robin the Not-Quite-So-Brave-As-Sir-Lancelot and Sir Galahad the Pure. On the way, Arthur battles the Black Knight who, despite having had all his limbs chopped off, insists he can still fight. They reach Camelot, but Arthur decides not to enter, as "it is a silly place".

programme.title: Idiocracy
programme.desc: Corporal Joe Bauers, a decidedly average American, is selected for a top-secret hibernation program but is forgotten and left to awaken to a future so incredibly moronic that he's easily the most intelligent person alive.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: Home Alone
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Kevin McCallister makes the most of the situation after his family unwittingly leaves him behind when they go on Christmas vacation. When thieves try to break into his home, he puts up a fight like no other.

programme.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off
programme.desc: A brash, cocky high school senior, tired of skipping school to spend a boring day at home, is determined to enjoy an epic day roaring around his favorite Chicago sites, enlisting his best friend and girlfriend to join him on the adventure.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Monty Python's Life of Brian
programme.desc: Brian Cohen is an average young Jewish man, but through a series of ridiculous events, he gains a reputation as the Messiah. When he's not dodging his followers or being scolded by his shrill mother, the hapless Brian has to contend with the pompous Pontius Pilate and acronym-obsessed members of a separatist movement. Rife with Monty Python's signature absurdity, the tale finds Brian's life paralleling Biblical lore, albeit with many more laughs.

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: When the nefarious Dark Helmet hatches a plan to snatch Princess Vespa and steal her planet's air, space-bum-for-hire Lone Starr and his clueless sidekick fly to the rescue. Along the way, they meet Yogurt, who puts Lone Starr wise to the power of "The Schwartz." Can he master it in time to save the day?

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop
programme.desc: Fast-talking, quick-thinking Detroit street cop Axel Foley has bent more than a few rules and regs in his time, but when his best friend is murdered, he heads to sunny Beverly Hills to work the case like only he can.

programme.title: Friday After Next
programme.desc: Craig and Day Day have finally moved out of their parents houses and into their own crib. The cousins work nights at a local mall as security guards. When their house is robbed on Christmas Eve they team up to track him down.

programme.title: The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad!
programme.desc: A rerun of many of the gags from the television series Police Squad! (1982). An Airplane! (1980)-type spoof, this time with the an incompetent Lieutenant Frank Drebin, who always "gets his man". Visual gags come thick and fast, and it's impossible to catch them all with one viewing. The plot: Queen Elizabeth II of England is coming to town, and Vincent Ludwig has plans to assassinate her using a brainwashed baseball player.

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training
programme.desc: After it's announced that one of the two police academies in the state will be closed down, Lassard's alumni return to the school to train new recruits and compete against the other academy.

programme.title: Naked Gun 33 1/3: The Final Insult
programme.desc: Frank Drebin is persuaded out of retirement to go undercover in a state prison. There he has to find out what top terrorist, Rocco, has planned for when he escapes. Adding to his problems, Frank's wife, Jane, is desperate for a baby.

programme.title: The Naked Gun 2½: The Smell of Fear
programme.desc: Lieutenant Frank Drebin discovers that his ex-girlfriend's new beau is involved in a plot to kidnap a scientist who advocates solar energy.

programme.title: Brewster's Millions
programme.desc: A minor-league baseball player must spend $30 million in 30 days in order to inherit $300 million. However, he's not allowed to own any assets, destroy the money, gift it, give it to charity, or tell anyone about the deal.

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: A man is murdered. Two men witness it. A blind man who hears the killer, and a deaf man who sees them. The police don't think they're credible witnesses, but the killers don't want to take any chances. The two men must now work together to save themselves and bring the killers to justice.

programme.title: Halloween III: Season of the Witch
programme.desc: After a terrified toy salesman is mysteriously attacked and brought to the hospital, clutching the year's most popular Halloween mask, Dr. Daniel Challis sets out to investigate the strange events and finds himself thrust into a nightmarish conspiracy.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: House of Dracula
programme.desc: A scientist working on cures for rare afflictions, such as a bone softening agent made from molds to allow him to correct the spinal deformity of his nurse, finds the physical causes of lycanthropy in wolf-man Larry Talbot and of vampirism in Count Dracula, but himself becomes afflicted with homicidal madness while exchanging blood with Dracula.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: An American who sells cars in England receives a mysterious invitation from an old, eccentric millionaire to visit his house in which he lives with his twin brother.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Invaders from Mars
programme.desc: A young boy learns that space aliens are taking over the minds of earthlings.

programme.title: Invisible Agent
programme.desc: The Invisible Man's grandson uses his secret formula to spy on Nazi Germany.

programme.title: The Rolling Stones Live From Rio
The Eagles - Don Kirshner's Rock Concert (1974)
G Love and Special Sauce - Woodstock 99 Act 03
Don Kirshner's Rock Concert - Steppenwolf-Ufo-Bad Company-Foghat Live (1974)
Oingo Boingo Live At The Ritz (1985)
John Lennon and the plastic ono band Toronto 1969
Van Halen - Live Without a Net (1986)
The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy's daughter, Kate is coming for a visit and has a big announcement. Dorothy is excited when she thinks the announcement is going to be that Kate is pregnant. However, when Kate arrives, her announcement is far from exciting. Meanwhile, Blanche and Rose stay up and watch an "I Love Lucy" marathon.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Sophia runs away to her old rest home. When the other girls try to get her, they find that it is no longer anywhere near as bad as she said it was.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Donna's on again off again relationship is back on and he invites her to dinner. Later, Fred gets a visit from a saleslady who peaks his interest and he blindly invites her to dinner, forgetting all about Donna. Thanks to Lamont's manipulations, he makes sure Fred has to juggle the dates around, keeping up appearances.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike accidently breaks Archie's Chair.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike asserts his independence by leaving Gloria behind while he joins his friends on a weekend skiing trip.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie is shocked when his coworker Stretch has died after cavorting with a young woman. Asked to give the eulogy, he enlists Mike's help, requesting Bible references. But the biggest shock comes at the funeral.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Caitlin & Brian vs. Lori & Val vs. Cynthia & Gary
programme.desc:
Sweep of Champions, Day 1
Clips from this episode were later seen on VH1's "I Love the '90s: Part Deux"
Caitlin & Brian is the same year in November 12, 1991. Lori & Val is the same year in November 13, 1991.
Gary & Cynthia is the same year in October 23, 1991.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: To inherit a fortune, voice teacher Shemp must marry before six o'clock, but no girl will accept his proposal. Finally one of his repulsive students agrees to marry him, just in the nick of time. When the rest of the prospective brides hear about the inheritance, they show up at the ceremony and a free for all ensues. Shemp marries his student before the deadline, and then finds out that there is no inheritance. Moe and Larry have tricked him into marriage as revenge for their marrying his shrewish sisters.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges go out west for Shemp's health. The boys soon run afoul of a local villain who is forcing pretty Nell to marry him. The bad guy has Nell's sisters locked up, and its up to the stooges to rescue them and save the day.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are pharmacists who invent a fountain of youth formula that can turn old people young. They turn an old lady into a beautiful young woman, but when her husband takes the formula it turns him into a gorilla.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: A follow up to "You Nazty Spy", the stooges have taken over the country of Moronica. Moe is Hailstone the Dictator, Curly is a Field Marshal and Larry is Minister of Propaganda. The stooges are planning with their allies to conquer the world, which mainly consists of fighting over a globe. The former king's daughter gets into their headquarters and plants a bomb which Curly detonates. All ends well as the king regains control of the country and the stooges wind up as trophies on the wall.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: A sequel (sort of) to "Hoofs and Goofs", The stooges are taking care of their sister Birdie who has been reincarnated as a horse. When they learn that her mate "Schnapps", a famous circus horse, is about to be destroyed, they got to the circus grounds to rescue him. The stooges are successful, and Birdie and Schnapps are reunited.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Three Stooges are gypped out of their inheritance by Icabod Slipp, crooked lawyer. The Boys follow Slipp onto a train and corner him, but not before they let a lion loose on the train.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The King of Moronica is overthrown and the Three Stooges are chosen to take his place. When three politicians from a small country discover there is no money in peace, they decide to hire a paperhanger (Moe) as a puppet dictator. Classic Stooge short was the first Hollywood film to satirize the Nazis and Fascists from World War II (predated Charlie Chaplin’s “The Great Dictator” by 9 months). Due to its historical significance, this was known as the favorite Stooge short of Larry, Moe and Jules White. Much of the short reflects the American Public’s knowledge of affairs in Germany at that time. First Stooge appearance by Stooge supporting player John Tyrrell.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: Terror strikes the London Opera House as a new opera is disrupted by the actions of a deformed specter of the show's past

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky is suspended for allegedly making an obscene gesture in her class photo which makes her suddenly popular at school, but Roseanne is the one called into the principal's office.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan ill-advisedly tells Roseanne he's having dreams of a mysterious, and oddly homely, other woman.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: Do You Know Where Your Parents Are?
programme.desc: Since the girls continually break curfew, Dan and Roseanne celebrate the anniversary of their first lovemaking by revisiting the restaurant/hotel and staying out very, very late, hoping to teach the girls a lesson, with the help of Officer Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: An angry and deflated Roseanne discovered that her mother always thought sister Jackie had more potential.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: The Courtship of Eddie, Dan's Father
programme.desc: Dan's visiting father astonishes everyone by announcing his plans to marry Crystal.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Part 2 of 2. Roseanne throws Crystal a bridal shower and discovers yet another secret. Ed desperately tried to bond with Crystal's son Lonnie. Dan vehemently opposes this wedding, and is furious with Roseanne for participating in it against his wishes. Convinced that his father will ruin Crystal's life, Dan confronts the man in hopes of getting him to call the wedding off.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky runs away from home to live with Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Darlene is embarassed when Roseanne gives Darlene's home economics class a supermarket lesson in real-life family budgeting; D.J. has to deal with a bully.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Ms. Brahms appears in a TV commercial, but she's made to cover up her high-pitched cockney accent with a sophisticated voice stand-in. When a man wants to go on a date with her, has the voice switch ruined Ms. Brahms' chances?

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.sub-title: The Erotic Dreams of Mrs. Slocombe
programme.desc: Mrs. Slocombe, who has been a widow for quite a long time, begins to have weird erotic fantasies about Mr. Humphries. Her cranky behavior towards everybody else, including her customers, forces Mr. Rumbold to intervene.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill and Toony salute the troops with patriotic cartoons!
Featured cartoons: Draftee Daffy (1945) The Draft Horse (1942) Wacky Blackout (1942) Ration Fer the Duration (1943) Falling Hare (1943)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: A popular country western singer wants to get rid of his religiously zealous wife who's been using blackmail to control every aspect of his life. But a murder made to look like an airplane accident does not fool the wily Lt. Columbo.

programme.title: Maude
programme.sub-title: Walter's Problem (1) (a.k.a.) Life of the Party (1)
programme.desc: After a wild evening of drinking, Maude, Walter and Arthur awake to discover some oddities. First, Maude and Arthur shared a bed, second the guys trashed the living room and third, Walter made an obscene phone call to Maude's mother. Carol suggests that perhaps Walter has a drinking problem but he refuses to face that fact. Maude and Walter later get into a big argument while they are both drunk and Walter slaps her.

programme.title: Maude
programme.sub-title: Walter's Problem (2) (a.k.a.) Life of the Party (2)
programme.desc: After giving Maude a black eye, Walter continues to drink. Later, Arthur sends over a recovering alcoholic to talk some sense into Walter who adamantly refuses help. When Walter discovers Maude got rid of all the alcohol, he goes ballistic and she locks him in the closet. That's when Carol makes an announcement, she's taking Phillip and is moving out

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Two police detectives stake out the girls' home to investigate the neighbors who are believed to be dealers in stolen gems.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: While preparing for a friend's birthday party, the girls recall past birthdays, including Rose's surprise birthday party for Dorothy, a surprise birthday party for Blanche and a memory of Sophia's birthday involving her late-husband Sal.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: In this pilot for another series (Empty Nest, obviously the concept was retooled), the girls' friend Renee is feeling depressed and lonely with her children grown up and moved out and her husband's workaholic habits.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan journey to the premiere of No Holds Barred to present an extraordinary lineup of in-ring competition, featuring a main event contest between Hillbilly Jim and Greg "The Hammer" Valentine. Plus, a look at an unusual pizza delivery man living an American dream, The Twin Towers in action, and more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: When Gene finds an expensive talking toilet out in the woods, he and his friends decide to protect it as much as they can while an evil toilet hunter is looking for the toilet.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert Barone finds himself in the most unlikely position of having to choose one of three women-girlfriend Amy, ex-wife Joanne, or Italian bombshell Stefania - to share the future with him. Unfortunately, when he tells Amy about his recent reunion with ex-wife however, it all starts to come apart.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray inadvertently commits petslaughter on Ally's hamster while Debra and the kids are away for the weekend. When Ray breaks the difficult news to Ally about Pumpernickel's untimely demise, Debra insists that they give the beloved pet a proper burial, complete with eulogies, but Ray thinks that it's ridicules to morn a pet.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug Heffernan (played by Kevin James) finds Deacon Palmer (played by Victor Williams) out with another woman, Angie (played by Patricia McBride) and Deacon makes him promise not to say anything. When Carrie (played by Leah Remini) finds out, she begins to question their marriage because Doug and her apparently have different views on whether or not it is cheating.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After Deacon and Kelly's separation, Carrie begins to wonder about her own marriage. She and Doug agree to put their sex lives on hold for a few weeks because Carrie feels that they should resolve arguments verbally, not always physically. Meanwhile, Arthur dates a woman who claims she was once Frank Sinatra's girlfriend.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George gets Steinbrenner hooked on eggplant calzones from a local Italian restaurant. But George gets banned from the restaurant when he's accused of stealing from the tip jar. He then asks Kramer to pick up the calzones, but Kramer gets kicked out when he tries to pay with pennies.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry has an argument with his mechanic; Newman becomes obsessed with Michigan's recycling program; Elaine goes to an auction for her boss.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Conner family terrify each other out of their wits on Halloween night.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne tries to get a few minutes of relaxation in the bathtub, but she ends up getting constant interruptions from her family--and a fantasy about two hunks.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: As Kevin and the rest of the crew worry about Kevin's medical test results to see whether or not he has skin cancer, Michael gets angry because no one seems to have fun at the birthday party he threw for himself. Later, Michael takes everyone out to the skating rink to get their minds off of Kevin's possible complications, and Pam and Jim go to the supermarket to pick up some presents for Kevin.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Dwight plays the role of Volunteer Sheriff after finding half a joint in the Dunder Mifflin parking lot leading to an investigation. Pam gives Jim a play challenge where he must be silent until he gives Pam a can of Coke.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: When Michael takes over conflict resolution duties from HR, chaos ensues at Dunder Mifflin.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In this clip show episode, Homer is honored at a Friars' Club Roast. A number of characters show up to roast him, but the celebrating is interrupted by Kang and Kodos, who say that humanity will be judged based on Homer's experiences.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Inspired by a cartoonist who speaks at the school as part of a career day assembly, Bart creates a comic book series based on Homer and his anger problems, which turns into a popular Internet cartoon series called Angry Dad. Homer finds out about this and is at first outraged, but after talking to his family, he decides to try to become a less angry person.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge discover that Apu is having an affair with the Squishee delivery lady at the Kwik-E-Mart. They decide to keep Apu's wife Manjula from finding out about it. However, she eventually learns of Apu's affair by watching store security tapes. She throws Apu out of the house and decides to file for divorce, but soon realizes that she misses him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Little Girl in the Big Ten
programme.desc: Lisa tries to fit in with two college students by lying about her age. She finds that the college atmosphere is perfect for her, but her lie is soon discovered and she is shunned by her fellow elementary school students. Meanwhile, Bart is diagnosed with a weakened immune system after getting bitten by a Chinese mosquito and must live in a plastic, germ-free bubble.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While faced with community service for abusing an endangered insect, Homer begins assisting an elderly woman named Mrs. Bellamy. One night, Mrs. Bellamy is murdered, and Homer and Marge are accused of committing the crime.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: After finding a box of old shoes, Al and Jefferson turn the shoe store into a 70's themed retro shoe store.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud gets a job as a driving examiner and he is the examiner when Al goes to renew his long expired drivers license. Meanwhile, Marcy gets Kelly a job as the model window girl at her bank after Jefferson's egocentric, selfish advice makes Kelly lose her job as the Verminator.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly's zit ruins her beer commercial.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud's intense studying for a scholarship exam makes him periodically nod off and fantasize about making out with beautiful women. So, is a passionate encounter between him and Marcy's visiting, sexually aggressive, 19-year-old niece, Amber, a dream or real?

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: NO MA'AM counter-protests against Marcy and company when Al banishes a customer for nursing her baby. Meanwhile, train accidents delay Peggy's trip home.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The day after, with no end in sight to the feud and both sides unwilling to back down, Al concocts a plan to rid the shoe store of women by selling mens shoes. But will it hold up when the long-unseen owner Gary finally arrives? Al's case is blown to bits after Gary shows up.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Bud's community-service assignment for college is the one he is least suited for: consulting virgins on the brink of temptation; he must wrestle with temptation himself when he goes to one's house to talk her out of it. Meanwhile, Al and Peggy go to the video store to find a movie that they can watch together.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Jefferson learns that the Barbie doll he bought for Marcy on her birthday at an auction is worth a small fortune of $50,000. So he asks Al a big favor: sleep next to Marcy for the night while he goes out to exchange dolls. In return, Al gets to keep a prized first edition of the magazine "Big 'Uns" that he bought at the auction with Jefferson's money.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: Here Comes the Bride, There Goes the Bride
programme.desc: Engaged, Lamont is excited about his impending nuptials but on the wedding day he quickly finds himself the only one who is. The bride dumps him at the altar and he finds his relatives begging to get back their wedding gifts.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont buy a load of copper from a man that has been stealing copper in the neighborhood. The man is late for a meeting with Lamont at the diner because he is ripping off the Sanford plumbing pipes, which he then sells to Fred and Lamont.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: A Matter of Life and Breath
programme.desc: Lamont comes down hard on Fred to try to get him to quit smoking and has him go to the doctor for a chest x-ray. Later, the results are sent in the mail and Fred becomes convinced he has tuberculosis.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: While cleaning the house, Fred knocks over Lamont's glass figurine collection. Trying to cover up the fact that he broke them, Fred makes up a story about how the house was robbed and how he fought them off. In the process, he is labeled as a hero and Fred takes advantage of it.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris goes to work for the police in a sting operation after he buys a counterfeit watch, the Chrono-Sync 2000.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: At the family reunion Chris is still struggling to get out from under the shadow of his cousin.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Pirate Yosemite Sam chases Bugs all over a pirate ship to find out where the buried treasure is.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Yosemite Sam is a-lookin' for any varmint what dares to tame him. And Bugs is just the varmint.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs is tricked into being the first rabbit shot into space.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Not knowing Bugs will soon have a brain transplant with a chicken, a scientist treats Bugs like a king, making him feel comfortable enough to trust the mad scientist.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Foghorn Leghorn tries indignantly to prove he is a chicken to Little Henry the Chicken Hawk.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Elmer Fudd takes in Sylvester Cat and an orange kitten during a cold winter night. He'd like to adopt them both but can only keep one.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky and Sylvester arrive at their new home, which looks creepy. Sylvester is so frightened by the house that he sneaks into Porky's bedroom at night to sleep. Porky thinks the house is a charming old place. However, by the end of the cartoon, Sylvester gets exactly what he expected.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Elmer chases Bugs out of the woods, into the city, into a theatre.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Daria is assigned to help the boys with their science project.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys have Mr. Anderson's credit card, and buy $6,275 worth of animals at the pet store...which puts a damper on Anderson's Odd Fellows convention.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Van Driessen has Beavis and Butt-head clean his house to learn about work and money; instead they learn that 8-track tapes aren't waterproof.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head are recruited for an amateur game of football, the rules of which they don't understand.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Buzzcut orders the boys to take a shower after gym class, when suddenly the fire alarm goes off and they are forced to exit the building clad in only their skivvies.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head play frisbee with an antique plate belonging to Stewart's mom.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Fed up with their attempts to get into Todd's gang, Beavis and Butt-head try to form their own.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head head to Mexico with their friend, Dave, in an effort to obtain illegal fireworks, but instead make a run for the bathroom after a taco stand owner gets revenge on the duo who fed his dog hot sauce.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get jobs working at a computer tech support center, a position for which they are woefully under-qualified.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: There's a heat wave today "here" at the Playhouse. Judge Pee-wee Herman is set to "hear" the arguments in a trial pitting Miss Yvonne against Mrs. Rene after they both wear the same one-of-a-kind dress.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee has lost the Cowntess' pencil sharpener! While he searches to find it, he and the rest of the gang collect things from the Playhouse that they don't use anymore so that he can give them to charity. "Wait" until you see what the secret word is!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After Caroline has an incredible make-out session with Max's pastry school teacher, Chef Nicholas (recurring guest star GILLES MARINI), Caroline finds out something that makes her wish it never happened.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max finds herself wanting to be more than just friends with her pastry school partner, Deke. Meanwhile, Caroline is "hot for teacher" - the married Chef Nicholas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke takes Max to his place after a great first date, his "home" is nothing like she expected. Meanwhile, Caroline feels empowered - then scared for her life - after having a shady car towed from the front of their apartment building.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: On this edition of Prime Time Wrestling, Gorilla Monsoon and Bobby "The Brain" Heenan host an extraordinary lineup of in-ring competition, including Bad News Brown going one-on-one with Brutus "The Barber" Beefcake in a main event confrontation. Plus, The Ultimate Warrior in action, another look at The American Dream Dusty Rhodes, and more.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Daemon and the Sea Snake battle the Crabfeeder. The realm celebrates Aegon's second nameday. Rhaenyra faces the prospect of marriage.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Robb and Catelyn arrive at Riverrun for Lord Hoster Tully's funeral. Tywin names Tyrion the new Master of Coin. Arya says goodbye to Hot Pie. The Night's Watch returns to Craster's. Brienne and Jaime are taken prisoner.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: And Now His Watch Is Ended
programme.desc: The Night's Watch takes stock. Varys meets his better. Arya is taken to the commander of the Brotherhood. Dany exchanges a chain for a whip.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A victim of a mugging presents with neurological symptoms and begins to mirror the behaviors of his doctors. Foreman joins the new fellows in seeking a diagnosis; Cameron and Chase takes bets on whom House will fire next.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Based on practically no information and no medical history about a mystery patient sent by the CIA, House is using some unorthodox methods to diagnose and treat him. Meanwhile the remaining candidates are questioning Foreman's judgment.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Bart become father–son con artists (after Bart is given money out of pity when Homer decided to not take him home), but soon find that they are the ones being had.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A snowstorm traps the students of Springfield Elementary inside, with them overthrowing Principal Skinner when he uses his Army skills to control them. Meanwhile, Homer tries to rescue the children–using Flanders' car.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While working as a human guinea pig (to pay off the family's lost savings after making a bad investment), Homer discovers the root cause of his subnormal intelligence: a crayon that was lodged in his brain ever since he was a boy. He decides to have it removed to increase his IQ, but discovers that being smart does not necessarily equal being happy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge is responsible for getting Jack Crowley,a prisoner with artistic talent, parole and a job at the school,painting a vast and beautiful mural. However Skinner dislikes it and orders a twee replacement. Both the new mural and Skinner's car are consequently torched and Marge is dismayed to think that Jack let her down. Homer experiences a similar lack of success when he tries to market a trash can as a cure for back pains.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Milhouse are banned from The Android Dungeon after stopping Comic Book Guy from buying a box of priceless Star Wars memorabilia for $5, but are hired as his replacements when Comic Book Guy has a heart attack and is advised to leave his job in order to make friends.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: While making funeral arrangements for Grampa's future, the funeral planner pitches the idea of a mausoleum that uses the same amount of cement as a full-sized tennis court. This prompts Homer to instead build a tennis court in his backyard, initially mistaking the sport for "Foxy Boxing". The tennis court makes Marge and Homer popular in town, but it turns out that this is only because Homer's inferior tennis ability makes them an easy target. Bart on the other hand, shines as a natural.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty announces his retirement due to interference from network executives and the growing popularity of the big-money game show "Me Wantee", but when Krusty reveals to Sideshow Bob that all of the episodes featuring him have been erased, Sideshow Bob uses Krusty's biggest fan (Bart) to murder him during Krusty's farewell show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart, Nelson, Millhouse, and Ralph are recruited by a man named L.T. Smash to be in a boy band called the Party Posse but when Lisa discovers the true identity of LT. Smash as a navy recruitment officer she tries to tell Homer and Marge with hilarious results.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a Good Samaritan after discovering the simple joys of helping people in need–which is put to the test when he goes on a hunger strike after the owner of the Springfield Isotopes attempts to discredit him when Homer stumbles on his plot to discreetly move the team to Albuquerque, New Mexico.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Lisa becomes the target of a female bully, Lisa discovers a scientific reason as to why bullies pick on nerds. Meanwhile, Homer goes into the baby-proofing business, which is threatening the businesses of pediatricians and greeting card salesmen.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: Following "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's reinstatement, Mr. McMahon is stunned to realize his rivalry with Austin has become a family matter. However, Ken Shamrock looks to force The Rattlesnake back out of the company when they meet in an "I Quit" Match. Plus, the New Age Outlaws defend the Tag Team Championship against Al Snow & Mankind and more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: When David contemplates starting a new business in town, Moira uses her seat on town council to protect her son's interests. Alexis discovers high school isn't as easy as it used to be. Johnny reluctantly offers romantic advice to Ivan, the hapless baker, in return for help with the motel's continental breakfast.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: David meets a new friend while setting up his business, Johnny defends Alexis’s schoolwork, and Moira deals with a difficult guest at the motel.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline help launch the new branch of The High at JFK airport, and Sophie discovers that Oleg may have an ulterior motive for wanting to marry her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are tasked with retrieving Sophie’s wedding dress from customs and safely transporting it to the church in time for her big wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline team up with the diner staff in a frantic attempt to keep their businesses open when the city targets Han's diner and their cupcake window for destruction. Also, Sophie and Oleg announce that they are trying to have a baby.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When the shower at Max and Caroline's apartment breaks, they get part-time jobs at a fancy gym's juice bar and enjoy free use of the locker room. However, their jobs and free showers are jeopardized when Caroline has a run-in with a group of rich moms who are jealous of the attention she's getting from a sexy trainer.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline's ex Andy returns and she is denial over how they broke up. And when he informs her that he's about to get married, she offers to make the wedding cake. And she is constantly wondering if she made a mistake breaking up with him. And she tries to find wrong with him but can't.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's cupcake shop is boycotted after they refuse to sell cupcakes to "I", an artist who wants to put cocktail weenies in them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han insists the diner gang attends a mandatory night out in an "Escape Room Club" when he feels they aren't working together as a team. However, once they're locked inside the "Alice in Wonderland"-themed room, they find Han has an ulterior motive.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Not Regular Down There
programme.desc: Max is both puzzled and curious when the new guy she is dating expresses hesitations about intimacy because he is "not regular down there." Also, Sophie and Oleg struggle to get pregnant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline's grandmother Astrid awakens from a coma with no knowledge of her family's massive money scandal, Caroline attempts to keep the truth hidden for a bit longer by giving Astrid one last fancy luncheon.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Oleg's pro-basketball playing cousin comes to town to play a game against Max's favorite team and gifts her with two free tickets, but chaos ensues when the girls interfere with the game and risk ruining their cupcake business' reputation in the process.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Earl is not invited to play with his old jazz band at their reunion show, Max and the diner gang join forces in order to get him back on stage performing.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max reconnects with an old friend, Caroline’s jealousy inspires her to try to make some new friends of her own.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline sign up to be "booth babes" in order to get into a huge gaming convention for free. Once inside, they're shocked to see that Han's friend has created video game characters based on them, and demand compensation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline shares her riches-to-rags life story on stage at a storytelling night and captures the attention of a Hollywood studio executive. Meanwhile, Sophie and Oleg search for a surrogate.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max tags along with Caroline as she travels to Los Angeles on a film studio’s dime to discuss the idea of making her riches-to-rags life story into a movie. While there, Max loses her luggage but finds a new crush.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline continues meeting with studio writers in an effort to make her life story into a movie, but she is torn when they reveal they would not portray Max in the project. Also, Max is being spoiled by her new Hollywood beau, Randy, who sets Caroline up on a date with his friend, Bob.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: It's Ted's first day of coaching, and fans aren't happy. He makes little headway but remains undeterred as the team plays its first match.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: To arrange an in-depth exposé, Rebecca pairs cynical journalist Trent Crimm with Ted for a day. Ted and Roy venture into the community.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Rebecca hosts the team's annual charity benefit, where Ted stages a reconciliation between Roy and Jamie.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Phil Leotardo seeks to continue a cycle of retaliation between the New York and Jersey families. Christopher wrestles with his addictions. Tony decides keeping Carmela occupied with the spec house is for the best. A.J. gets a new girlfriend.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Bobby and Janice host a wild 47th birthday bash for Tony at their Adirondacks vacation home. Tony's actions following Johnny Sack's arrest come back to haunt him.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Arriving at a new place, Michael receives an unexpected honor. Meanwhile, the rest of the group explores — and learns a shocking truth.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra continues her seemingly futile search for a suitable match, Daemon returns Westeros to Kings Landing and stirs more trouble for the King. Rhaenyra learns a valuable lesson about desires and what it means to be a daughter of a King.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When the Wattersons arrive at the mall for a day of shopping, they struggle to find a space in the parking lot. They search for a space everywhere -- using all their intelligence, cunning and trickery -- but as the day goes on, the desperate hunt for a space becomes more important than the trip to the mall itself.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard's trip to the store to find mayonnaise for the family's burger night turns into a Neverending Story-meets-Conan the Barbarian-style quest where he squares off against Tina Rex's father, Mr. Rex.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin see an advert for the latest, cutting-edge Bobert operating system and excitedly talk Bobert into downloading it. At first it seems like a massive improvement, but the many flaws in the new operating system become increasingly apparent.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Sarah creates a comic of Gumball as a superhero named LaserHeart, prompting Gumball to act like one in real life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Fearing that she may be sick of him due to a missing emoji on her message to him, Gumball sends Penny on a romantic quest to rekindle their love, which turns into a disaster when Penny gets lost in the woods.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball goes onto Elmore Stream-It, but is immediately interrupted by Darwin's protest. Gumball begs Darwin to just let him see one video, but Darwin continues. He gets Gumball to back away from the computer and turn around, revealing that he has the mouse in his hand.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball sets out to prove to Penny's father that he's good enough for his daughter. Gumball decides that the best way to prove to Mr. Fitzgerald that he's good enough for Penny is to become his apprentice for the day. The problem is, Mr. Fitzgerald has an important interview and Gumball's enthusiastic attempts to help him might actually do more harm than good, both to Mr. Fitzgerald's business and to Gumball's future with Penny.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: To prove to Darwin that he can be unpredictable, Gumball decides to hug the first person he sees -- an anthropomorphic hot dog -- and Gumball ends up in over his head when he and the Hot Dog Guy can't stop bumping into each other.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After being framed for feeding coffee- and prune juice-laced nuts to the squirrels, Darwin tries to prove to Gumball that Mr. Robinson's wife, Margaret, is a good person underneath her destructive, sociopathic tendencies, but when Darwin sees just how evil Margaret is, the duo decide to catch her committing grand theft auto.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Alan allegedly ditches Gumball's dinner date with him, Gumball is desperate to track him down and make him pay for his betrayal, only to learn that Alan is taking his mother, Jessica, to the hospital for an organ transplant.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin, Beastboy and Cyborg decide to have a crazy Boys' Night Out without Starfire. Starfire brings Raven along to break Jinx out of jail to have a Girl's Night Out ; Beast Boy's indecisiveness causes him to be fired from the Teen Titans and the Teen Titans have to find a replacement Titan member, ranging from Vixen, B'wana Beast, Detective Chimp, and the Wonder Twins.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy's behavior causes him to be fired from the Teen Titans and replaced by a pair of shape-shifting twins.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Envious of the other Titans' superpowers following their fight with the H.I.V.E. Five (consisting of Mammoth, Gizmo, Jinx, Billy Numerous, and See-More), Robin wants some of his own. After a disastrous scientific experiment goes awry that merges him with the DNA of the American Robin, he convinces Raven to use her magic to give him superpowers even though she claims that superpowers are a curse.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg plugs into the control panel of the Titans tower and drives the others crazy.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Starfire's new pet is a horribly dangerous parasite, but Robin is the only one who is suspicious of the beast.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Starfire is the only Titan invited to the Titans East Annual Dance Party, Beast Boy teaches her how to lie so that she can spare the other Titans' feelings - but her lies turn all of the Titans against each other.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: While trying to entice Raven into joining a meatball party, Cyborg fires a half-meat, half-robot mystery meatball into her mouth, cracking her tooth and releasing the demon octopus that lives in her mouth.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Robin's long bo-staff is accidentally broken by the other four Titans, he seeks a new one at the mystical Tree of the Universe.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy thinks the villainess Terra is his new girlfriend, but she is just using him to gain access to all of the Titans' secrets. Only Raven suspects Terra, but the others just think that Raven is jealous.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans confidently expect to win an upcoming dodgeball championship, even though the only thing they ever practice is their victory dance. Then they come up against an unbeatable dodge ball team - the H.I.V.E.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren is suffering from horrible allergies. Little does he know that he may be allergic to his best pal Stimpy.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy find an abandoned circus clown at their doorstep. While Ren DOES NOT want to keep him, Stimpy pleads and begs until Ren agrees.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy get jobs as bellhops and must make sure that no one finds out who is living in the top two floors of the hotel, but their curiosity gets the best of them.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy starts his journey into teenagedom, complete with all of its trials & tribulations.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren thinks TV has ruined Stimpy's mind, but when Stimpy wins a televised Gritty Kitty Litter contest and becomes a Hollywood star on the Muddy Muddskipper show, he leaves Ren behind.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head can't handle the truth! After witnessing a brutal beating that leaves one man out cold, will the boys lie under oath to protect their idol Todd? If they screw up, there's a serious butt-kicking on the docket, Todd-style.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get trapped in an elevator with an attractive woman and do all they can to escape.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Peace, Love and Understanding is the second pilot episode of Beavis and Butt-head, after "Frog Baseball". Like creator Mike Judge's other early short cartoons, it was funded by Spike and Mike and shown on MTV's Liquid Television in the early 1990s. Mike Judge stated in an interview that he heavily disliked this particular episode, along with the season 1 episode "Give blood" which were both conceived on the same day.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Jello Swirl Pops (Jenny & Jay) vs. Big Toes (Jamie & Matt)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Dream Warriors (Maria & Sean) vs. Supersonic Brainstorms (Coleen & Dave)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Rogers Gets Evicted will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine's family moves into a new house in the hills, a kid on wheels from California catches the attention of his new classmates.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Electric Company Blows a Fuse will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast takes another look at computers and robots. In the process, Ross tests out an interactive poll system. Viewers are supposed to touch the superimposed green and red squares on the screens to determine what happens next. The result: in the space of four minutes, Luke gets shot, Vanessa is slimed, and Lisa is forced to not say anything.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Dallas & Dynasty will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast explains their ambitions in life. Christine wants to grow as an actress and begs Ross to provide her with a meaty scene within the show. Ben, meanwhile, wants Ross to adjust the budget so he can star in his own music video.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Mickey Mouse Club will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine joins a health club, but isn't really interested in the club's healthy atmosphere and Ross involves the kids in an investment club with himself as their broker.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The A-Team Wreck Disneyland will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast tries to convince Ross to give them a holiday.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The college football game scheduled at this time between MIT and UCLA will not be seen because "mitucla" is a dirty word in Arundi Barundi.
Among the cast's plans for college, Alasdair plans to enter on a video game scholarship but, so far, can't find a university to meet his aim. Ross returns to college so he can enter a profession without kids around.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Boy George Without Makeup will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Christine puts a spell on Ross that causes evil ghosts to haunt him.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rudolph the Red Nosed Reindeer Joins the A-Team will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
It's Christmas on the set, but there's little peace on Earth in the studio: Alasdair's running around with a helmet camouflaged in mistletoe and two eager lips puckered.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
To Tell the Truth (with guest host Richard Nixon) will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Lisa is nominated by Christine to run for class president.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Today, it's oil slicks and bad flicks as NASCAR takes on Hollywood's Box Office Bombs. It's the classic battle of fast cars vs. 'Ishtars.' Games - Drop Dead Line, Muddy Runs, Chum in the Mouth, and Battlefeltch Earth.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's vaporizers and exercisers as celebrated Potheads take on the Fitness Industry...it's the classic battle of degenerate Stoners versus strong Boners. Games - Bagel bumpers, Wallbangers, Circle Jerkers & Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's Churchfolks & Queer as Folks as the Religious Right takes on Gay Rights. It's the classic battle of bible stories vs. fairy tales. Games - Amazing Grace, Sinkers & Floaters, Dope on a Rope & Rotating Surfboard of Death.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Today, it's crime fighters and kilobiters as world famous superheroes take on the world of MySpace... It's the classic battle of Heroes and Freaks vs. emailing geeks. Games - Slippery Slope of Death, Dash to Death, Log Drop & Pole Riders.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Sexual Pioneers vs. The World of Violent Films
programme.desc: It's Night stalkers and Silk Stockings, as Violent Movies takes on Sexual Pioneers and Lingerie. It's the classic battle of Scarfacers versus the leather and lacers. Games - Great Holes of Glory, Log Drop, One & One Make #2 & Irritable Bowl Syndrome.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's hard lovin' and hard labor as celebrated chick magnets take on famous felons. It's the classic battle of ladies mens vs. state pens. Games - Cut the Cheese, Sinkers & Floaters, Dead End Zone, Eat Shitake.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's punch lines and bungee cords, as the stars of comedy take on the stars of "Jackass." It's the classic battle of non-stop yuks vs. stapled nuts. Games - Sprayed and Neutered, Boulder Dash, Door Jammed & Rotating Surfboard of Death.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: The Young and Rich vs. Men's Magazines
programme.desc: It's idle money and sexy bunnies as the young and rich take on the men's magazine industry. It's the classic battle of spoiled jetsetters vs. Penthouse petters. Games - Rump Bumpers, Hand Job, Dash to Death & Pole Riders.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A football injury sidelines star quarterback Kevin, forcing him to re-evaluate his life for a minute or two.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When a history test is stolen, Daria alone can solve the crime - provided she doesn't wake up.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Inspired by a lecture and the tribulations of Thomas Edison and the Wright Brothers, Mr. O'Neill encourages his class to pick something to fail at for personal growth. All his pupils get out of it instead are misery, grief, and despair, including Daria who may be forced to supervise Quinn at an upcoming mall fashion exhibit, and Jane, who's becoming so popular that she might replace Brittany as... a CHEERLEADER!!

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: While struggling to buy toilet paper for Jake, Daria gets stranded at the Lawndale High homecoming parade, where she meets Jane who plans to meet Tom so they can make fun of everybody. Instead, she runs into Tad Gupty who lost contact with his family(see "Pinch Sitter," Episode #1.8) and then Tom, who she finds is not such a bad guy after all. In fact, he may be more.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Helen has to cancel out of a business junket with Jake, guess who is coerced into taking her place?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The Morgendorffers visit a "spa for the soul" to get their psyches massaged.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A labyrinthine discount-bulk-warehouse superstore serves as the destination for several characters. Jane goes there to buy Gummi Bears (for art supply purposes) after Tom ate hers. Daria accompanies her to buy new shoelaces, because Quinn stole the ones from her boot to repair a bag. Trent and Jesse go there to buy a headlamp and scented candles, respectively, and Tom accompanies them so he can find Jane and make up with her after their argument. (Walking with the two musicians, their half-brained purchase ideas drive him nearly up the wall.) The Fashion Club goes to the warehouse store to buy sunscreen and various other beauty needs, and, of course, their incredibly high standards prevent them from making any actual purchases. The free-sample cheese logs at the store become quite popular-an obsession, in fact, for hungry Mr. DeMartino, whose food budget is incredibly tight, and for Mrs. Johansen (a 300-pound hypoglycemic woman who is a recurring character). Kevin's dad, Doug, is holdi

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Is Lawndale a pretty little suburb, or an unholy nexus of evil haunted by the tortured denizens of the Beyond?

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has persuaded Count Duckula to visit London. Desperate for a TV show of his own, Duckula infects the entire government with his own showbiz sickness so they will agree to his every demand. After capturing Danger Mouse and Penfold, the evil vampire duck subjects them to the absolute worst comedy variety show ever to grace a theater stage. Despite of this, Duckula somehow managed to get his own cartoon series before the decade was over.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A Demon from the Fourth Dimension appears on Danger Mouse's doorstep to challenge the one-eyed white wonder to a duel. Before they can duke it out on the Demon's home turf however, the world's greatest secret agent is sidetracked or rather suckered into his own TV spectacular: 'This Was Your Life'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Proffesor Von Squakenkluck has invented a time machine and accidentally sends Penfold back 150 Million Years into the past in place of a dinosaur egg. Danger Mouse follows in the Mark 12, after changing places with a Diplodocus.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: DM and Penfold have just retrieved a stolen secret fuel sample from Field Marchall Rommole. On their way back, the Mark 5 gets hit by an electric storm, Penfold is struck dumb and the two of them are shot into a distant future where Britain cats are the dominant species. Worse still, a distant relative of Baron Greenback rules the cats under the name of 'Big Leo'.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Four Heads Are Better than Two
programme.desc: Colonel K has had his people create robot duplicates of Dangermouse and Penfold, so he can team the actual DM with robot Penfold and vice versa, in order to be able to stop more crime at the same time. The real Dangermouse and the fake Penfold are send to meet master of disguise Agent 57 at the Pinball Palace, where arch villain Baron Greenback is hatching a scheme to hypnotize the world by way of pinball machines.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: While climbing the rock of Gibraltar, Dangermouse and Penfold spot Baron GB's Frog Head Flyer and follow it to New York. There, they enter the International Oddbods Inc, an agency which represents some of the world's greatest crack-pots.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Homemade Volcano, Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Walk Through Paper, Heat Spiral and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Homemade Volcano. Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Heat Spiral, Heat From your Body, Paper Fold, Computer Plane and Walk Through Paper.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Bird Flies, Bleach Art, Tricks with Gravity, Fire Extinguisher and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Fish Bombing Run, How a Bird Flies, Impossible Holes, Bleach Art, Woolly Aphids, Tricks with Gravity, Computer Explosions, and Home Made Fire Extinguishers.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Flour, Pendulum, Leaf Color, How a Siren Works, Blow out Candle, Guitar, Aurora, Crush can and more
programme.desc: n this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Flour Explosion, Pendulum, Hidden Leaf Color, How Sirens Work, Mapping a Guitar, Blown Out Candle, Card Illusion, Aurora Borealis, Come Back Can and Crush Gallon Can.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Speed of Sound, Refraction, Trick, Light Bulb Filament, Glass Duet, Spell Check, Stars and more
programme.desc: Speed of Sound, Selective Refraction Trick, How Many Stars, Glass Duet, Spelling Checker, After a Forest Fire, Window Oscillation, Asparagus, Finding a Tree's Height. Mr. Wizard measures the difference between hearing the sound of a starter pistol over the walkie-talkie and hearing it through the air.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains how blood provides food and oxygen to the human body and removes the blood cells' waste products while defending your body from disease. He also covers questions on the speed of the fastest dog and the longest word, an experiment with salt and dreams and your imagination.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: In this episode, Beakman shows how plants "eat," reveals a host of creepy animal facts and shows how to make and recycle paper.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals how soap cleans and how explosions propel automobiles, and explores the question if birds sweat?

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals the truth on electrical currents, if carrots improve eyesight and explores the workings of the electric light.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman tells us about sound and how explosions are made.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Wetlands are not just home to different animals and plants, but they also control flooding and help keep the Earth’s water clean. Flood waters move more slowly through wetlands because they have to go through barriers of plants and dirt. Wetlands help stop erosion because the plants stop the soil from washing away. Plants and soil in wetlands slow water enough that bacteria and other decomposer organisms can break down and even absorb some types of pollution. Wetlands are wet. Let’s keep them that way.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Scientists believe that birds evolved from reptiles. Birds have backbones, and they lay eggs, but they’ve developed a unique feature that sets them apart from all other animals – feathers. Feathers are made of the same stuff human fingernails and hair are made from – a protein called keratin. Feathers, combined with lightweight bones, powerful wings and strong hearts let birds fly.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: In this, the first half-hour episode, a four-year-old Gumball becomes too much for young parents, Nicole and Richard, to handle, so they buy him a goldfish, but after the first six die from neglect, stupidity, and unfortunate incidents, Richard finds a special one in a shady red van who bonds with Gumball on a spiritual level. This gets put to the test when Darwin gets flushed down the toilet and must traverse a scary, new world to return home.
